Trial Designs and Population All pati

key_results: 14.2 Clinical Studies in Psoriatic Arthritis The psoriatic arthritis (PsA) clinical development program with tofacitinib tablets included 2 multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials in 816 adults with active PsA (Studies PsA-I and PsA-II).

Patients randomized and treated across the 2 clinical trials represented different PsA subtypes at screening, including <5 joints or asymmetric involvement (21%), ≥5 joints involved (90%), distal interphalangeal (DIP) joint involvement (61%), arthritis mutilans (8%), and spondylitis (19%).

key_results: 14.2 Clinical Studies in Psoriatic Arthritis The psoriatic arthritis (PsA) clinical development program with tofacitinib tablets included 2 multicenter, randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled trials in 816 adults with active PsA (Studies PsA-I and PsA-II).

key_results: 14.3 Psoriatic Arthritis The safety and efficacy of Enbrel were assessed in a randomized, double-blind, placebo-controlled study in 205 patients with PsA.
At 6 months, the ACR 20/50/70 responses were achieved by 50%, 37%, and 9%, respectively, of patients receiving Enbrel, compared to 13%, 4%, and 1%, respectively, of patients receiving placebo.

Responses increased over time, and at 6 months, the proportions of patients achieving a 50% or 75% improvement in the PASI were 47% and 23%, respectively, in the Enbrel group (N = 66), compared to 18% and 3%, respectively, in the placebo group (N = 62).

key_results: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The effectiveness of Acthar Gel as a treatment for infantile spasms was demonstrated in a single blinded (video EEG interpreter blinded) clinical trial in which patients were randomized to receive either a 2-week course of treatment with Acthar Gel (75 U/m 2 intramuscular twice daily) or prednisone (1 mg/kg by mouth twice daily).
Thirteen of 15 patients (86.7%) responded to Acthar Gel as compared to 4 of 14 patients (28.6%) given prednisone (p<0.002).
Seven of 8 patients (87.5%) responded to Acthar Gel after not responding to prednisone.

drugs.efficacy_snippet: 14 CLINICAL STUDIES The safety and efficacy of PYLARIFY were evaluated in two prospective, open-label, multi-center clinical studies in men with prostate cancer: OSPREY (NCT02981368) and CONDOR (NCT03739684). OSPREY OSPREY enrolled a cohort of 268 men with biopsy-proven prostate cancer who were cons

key_results: A total of 252 patients (94%) underwent standard-of-care prostatectomy and template pelvic lymph node dissection and had sufficient histopathology data for evaluation of the pelvic lymph nodes.
The total Gleason score was 7 for 19%, 8 for 46%, and 9 for 34% of the patients, with the remainder of the patients having Gleason scores of 6 or 10.
Approximately 24% of the evaluable patients had pelvic lymph node metastases based on histopathology (95% confidence interval: 19%, 29%).
Prior treatment included radical prostatectomy in 85% of the patients.

key_results: Subjects had a body surface area (BSA) involvement of ≥ 10%, a Psoriasis Area and Severity Index (PASI) score ≥ 12, and a static Physician’s Global Assessment (sPGA) ≥ 3 (moderate or severe).
In Trials PSO-1 and PSO-2, efficacy was assessed in 1,684 subjects randomized to either SOTYKTU (6 mg orally once daily), placebo, or apremilast (30 mg orally twice daily).
